title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
AFT
An Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) is an electronic transaction that is authorized by individuals to debit or credit their account.
Biller
A Biller is an entity that is set up on Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payments System to receive member-initiated Bill Payments.
Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP)
Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP) is the service provided by financial institutions pursuant to CPA Rule H6, Rules Pertaining to the Inter Financial Institution Exchange of Bill Payment Remittances for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement, which facilitates a
Corporate Creditor
having its Remittances accepted for payment at financial institutions.
BPRP
Bill Payment Remittance Processing.
Branch Number
The Branch Number is a 5-digit number that is used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number.
CCIN
A Corporate Creditor Identification Number (CCIN) is a non-transferable identification number assigned by the CPA to a Corporate Creditor. The CCIN uniquely identifies the
Corporate Creditor
for the purpose of Bill Payment Remittance Processing.
CCIN Bill Payment Agreement
A CCIN Bill Payment Agreement is a written agreement between Central 1 and its customers designating Central 1 as the lead bank for Bill Payment Remittance Processing for the specified CCIN. The agreement also sets out the terms and conditions for the provision of related services in accordance with CPA Rules and Standards. See Chapter 5, Signing a Bill Payment Remittance Processing Agreement, for details.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users canât manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
Corporate Creditor
A Corporate Creditor (also referred to as a Corporate Creditor Payee) is any business, association, government, or other entity that issues invoices to its customers and has entered into a Corporate Creditor Agreement for Bill Payment Remittance Processing with a payeeâs financial institution.
Corporate Creditor Agreement
A Corporate Creditor Agreement is a written agreement between a Corporate Creditor and a financial institution that designates Central 1 as the lead bank for the provision of Bill Payment Remittance Processing in connection with remittances using a particular CCIN. The agreement also sets out the terms and conditions for the provision of such services in accordance with CPA Rules and Standards.
CPA
The Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. CPS Admin Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications (e.g., Cash Services).
Direct Deposit
A Direct Deposit is an AFT credit transaction (e.g., a payroll deposit).
EDI
Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) is the electronic exchange of data in a structured computer message format, using public standards.
Electronic Bill Payment System
Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payments System allows participating financial institutions to submit member-initiated Bill Payments to Central 1 for further distribution to participating Billers.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
Applies to BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients.
Institution Number
An Institution Number is a 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that identifies the financial institution. The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number.
Invoice
An Invoice is a document issued by a Corporate Creditor to its customer as a request for payment. An Invoice generally consists of a Remittance and a Receipt.
OLT
The Online Tracing System is an application that allows authorized users to create bill payment trace requests and view Biller initiated trace requests.
Payeeâs Financial Institution
The Payeeâs financial institution is the financial institution that holds the account of the Corporate Creditor.
Payments Canada
Payments Canada is formerly known as the Canadian Payments Association (CPA) and was rebranded in 2016. Note that the legal name for Payments Canada is still the CPA.
Receipt
A Receipt is the portion of an Invoice that, if presented, is date-stamped by the Payorâs financial institution at the time of payment and can be retained by the Payor as proof of payment.
Remittance
A Remittance is the portion of the Invoice, or the electronic data, that is submitted to the Payorâs financial institution by the Payor along with the payment. The Remittance becomes a credit item representing value when it is exchanged by the Payorâs financial institution.
Restricted Entity
A Restricted Entity is an entity involved in one or more of the following:
Money services businesses, including currency exchanges
Money transmitters and casas de cambios, including payment services providers
Casinos and gambling establishments
Internet gambling/gaming associated businesses and websites
Marijuana providers and/or distributors
Virtual currency exchangers and administrators
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
Transit Number
A Transit Number is the 3-digit Institution Number together with the 5-digit Branch Number.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
END
